Title: The cytosolic aminotransferase VAS1 coordinates aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and metabolism

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; ics1 ics2 double mutant background
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid-associated pathway enzyme; product exported to cytosol pathway context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports precursor supply for VAS1-mediated 3-carboxy AAA production
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis using ics1 ics2 double mutant; targeted metabolite profiling; pathway inference
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS pathway supplies the precursor route for 3-carboxy AAA biosynthesis. 2. In the ics1 ics2 double mutant, 3-carboxyphenylalanine and 3-carboxytyrosine were undetectable. 3. The pathway branches from conserved isochorismate metabolism upstream of VAS1-dependent cytosolic reactions.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely competition with salicylic acid biosynthesis due to shared isochorismate precursor pool
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because mutant evidence in Results directly establishes metabolic dependence of target products on the isochorismate pathway, although ICS1/ICS2 are upstream context genes rather than the central focus compared with VAS1

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; ics1 ics2 double mutant background
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid-associated pathway enzyme; product exported to cytosol pathway context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis using ics1 ics2 double mutant; targeted metabolite profiling; pathway inference
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Together with ICS1, ICS2 is required for the isochorismate branch that feeds 3-carboxy AAA biosynthesis. 2. Double-mutant disruption abolished detectable 3-carboxyphenylalanine and 3-carboxytyrosine. 3. This places isochorismate upstream of VAS1-dependent metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports precursor supply for VAS1-mediated 3-carboxy AAA production
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely competition with salicylic acid biosynthesis due to shared isochorismate precursor pool
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results use the ics1 ics2 double mutant as direct evidence for precursor origin of the target metabolites, though ICS2 is not the principal focal gene

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and metabolism; cytosolic 3-carboxy aromatic amino acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic AAA recycling / 3-carboxy AAA branch derived from isochorismate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-carboxyphenylpyruvate; 3-carboxy-4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate; phenylpyruvate; 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate; 3-indolepyruvic acid; aromatic amino acids as amino donors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers amino groups between aromatic amino acids and aromatic keto acids; preferentially converts 3CPPY or 3CHPP plus AAA amino donors into 3-carboxyphenylalanine or 3-carboxytyrosine, and can also reversibly form Phe, Tyr, or Trp from PPY, HPP, or 3-IPA using amino acid donors such as Met.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; also recycles precursor pool for target amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript detected in 3-week-old seedlings and 6-week-old flowers; protein detectable in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels comparable among Col-0, adh2, and soa1; protein detectable in Col-0 and adh2 but absent in vas1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous 3-carboxyphenylalanine inhibits VAS1-dependent AAA biosynthesis in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of 3-carboxyphenylalanine and 3-carboxytyrosine while consuming aromatic amino acids; also supports cytosolic AAA biosynthesis/recycling and maintains AAA homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Suppressor genetics in adh2; loss-of-function mutants (vas1-1, vas1-2/soa1); overexpression; recombinant enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; metabolite profiling; immunoblotting; subcellular localization; isotope tracing
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VAS1 was identified as the causal suppressor gene of adh2, and loss of VAS1 partially rescues adh2 growth and AAA deficiency. 2. VAS1 directly catalyzes formation of 3-carboxyphenylalanine and 3-carboxytyrosine from 3CPPY/3CHPP using AAAs as amino donors in vitro and in vivo. 3. VAS1 also catalyzes cytosolic formation of Phe, Tyr, and Trp from aromatic keto acids, but strongly prefers 3-carboxy keto acid substrates. 4. The product 3-carboxyphenylalanine inhibits VAS1 AAA-biosynthetic activity, supporting feedback control of AAA homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole seedlings/plants; cytosol for biosynthetic activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Basic protein building blocks and precursors of diverse specialized metabolites essential for plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for metabolic engineering of AAA homeostasis and for generating 3-carboxy AAA-derived natural products; caution that overexpression lowers AAA levels and can aggravate growth defects in AAA-deficient backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: VAS1 overexpression decreases AAA levels and exacerbates dwarf phenotype in adh2; loss of function decreases 3-carboxy AAAs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The P174S mutation destabilizes or abolishes detectable protein accumulation and causes abnormal punctate cytoplasmic localization. VAS1-mediated 3-carboxy AAA synthesis is active in Arabidopsis and some Brassicaceae species. The enzyme was previously known for converting 3-IPA to Trp in auxin-related metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing of suppressor progeny; nontargeted metabolomics by UPLC-QTOF-MS; targeted metabolite profiling
